name: templates
context: Starter templates for each Dojo node type — ecosystem, standard, skill, context, and sub
info: Templates provide the starting skeleton for new Dojo nodes. Each of the five node types has a template with all required fields pre-filled and knowledge fields stubbed out. Templates are used by [[dojo/authoring/scaffold]] to generate new node directories and by human authors as copy-paste starting points. The templates encode best practices: correct field ordering, proper parent derivation from URI, type-appropriate field inclusion (scripts only for skill/sub, content_type only for context), and placeholder text that clearly marks what needs to be filled in.
triggers: what does an ecosystem node look like
body:
# Node Templates

Templates are the fastest way to create a correctly structured node. Each template is valid JSON that passes schema validation — the author's job is to replace the TODO placeholders with real content.

## Ecosystem Template

For top-level vendor/platform namespaces:

```json
{
  "name": "ECOSYSTEM_NAME",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "ECOSYSTEM_NAME",
  "type": "ecosystem",
  "context": "TODO: one-line description under 200 chars",
  "info": "TODO: dense paragraph with scope, execution surface, and why it matters",
  "parent": null,
  "tags": ["TODO"],
  "aliases": [],
  "triggers": [],
  "body": "",
  "sections": [],
  "links": [],
  "related": [],
  "more": {
    "docs": "TODO: official docs URL",
    "repo": "TODO: source repo URL"
  },
  "frontmatter": {
    "created": "DATE",
    "updated": "DATE",
    "author": "dojo-community",
    "audience": "TODO",
    "status": "living",
    "confidence": "high"
  },
  "author": "dojo-community",
  "license": "MIT",
  "created": "DATETIME",
  "updated": "DATETIME",
  "status": "draft"
}
```

## Standard Template

For protocols, specs, and conventions:

```json
{
  "name": "STANDARD_NAME",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "PARENT/STANDARD_NAME",
  "type": "standard",
  "context": "TODO: one-line description under 200 chars",
  "info": "TODO: dense paragraph with scope and why this standard matters",
  "parent": "PARENT_URI",
  "tags": ["TODO"],
  "aliases": [],
  "triggers": [],
  "body": "",
  "sections": [],
  "links": [],
  "related": [],
  "author": "dojo-community",
  "license": "MIT",
  "created": "DATETIME",
  "updated": "DATETIME",
  "status": "draft"
}
```

## Skill Template

For executable capabilities with scripts:

```json
{
  "name": "SKILL_NAME",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "PARENT/SKILL_NAME",
  "type": "skill",
  "context": "TODO: one-line description under 200 chars",
  "info": "TODO: dense paragraph with scope, execution surface, and why it matters",
  "parent": "PARENT_URI",
  "tags": ["TODO"],
  "aliases": [],
  "triggers": [],
  "body": "",
  "sections": [],
  "sub": [],
  "links": [],
  "related": [],
  "scripts": [
    {
      "id": "TODO-script-id",
      "name": "TODO: Script Name",
      "description": "TODO: what this script does",
      "lang": "bash",
      "runtime": "bash>=4",
      "entry": "./scripts/TODO.sh",
      "env": {},
      "packages": []
    }
  ],
  "schema": {
    "input": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {},
      "required": []
    },
    "output": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {}
    }
  },
  "depends": [],
  "author": "dojo-community",
  "license": "MIT",
  "created": "DATETIME",
  "updated": "DATETIME",
  "status": "draft"
}
```

## Context Template

For reference material, guides, and knowledge-only nodes:

```json
{
  "name": "CONTEXT_NAME",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "PARENT/CONTEXT_NAME",
  "type": "context",
  "content_type": "guide",
  "context": "TODO: one-line description under 200 chars",
  "info": "TODO: dense paragraph with scope and why this reference matters",
  "parent": "PARENT_URI",
  "tags": ["TODO"],
  "aliases": [],
  "triggers": [],
  "body": "",
  "sections": [],
  "links": [],
  "related": [],
  "frontmatter": {
    "created": "DATE",
    "updated": "DATE",
    "author": "dojo-community",
    "audience": "TODO",
    "estimated_reading_time": "TODO min",
    "status": "living",
    "confidence": "high"
  },
  "author": "dojo-community",
  "license": "MIT",
  "created": "DATETIME",
  "updated": "DATETIME",
  "status": "draft"
}
```

## Sub Template

For specialized variants of a skill:

```json
{
  "name": "SUB_NAME",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "PARENT_SKILL/SUB_NAME",
  "type": "sub",
  "context": "TODO: one-line description under 200 chars",
  "info": "TODO: dense paragraph focused on this specific variant",
  "parent": "PARENT_SKILL_URI",
  "tags": ["TODO"],
  "aliases": [],
  "triggers": [],
  "body": "",
  "sections": [],
  "links": [],
  "related": [],
  "scripts": [
    {
      "id": "TODO-script-id",
      "name": "TODO: Script Name",
      "description": "TODO: what this script does",
      "lang": "bash",
      "entry": "./scripts/TODO.sh",
      "env": {},
      "packages": []
    }
  ],
  "schema": {
    "input": { "type": "object", "properties": {} },
    "output": { "type": "object", "properties": {} }
  },
  "depends": [],
  "author": "dojo-community",
  "license": "MIT",
  "created": "DATETIME",
  "updated": "DATETIME",
  "status": "draft"
}
```

## Template Usage

1. Copy the template matching your node type
2. Replace all `TODO` and `UPPERCASE` placeholders
3. Derive `parent` from the URI (everything before the last `/`)
4. Fill in knowledge fields: aliases, triggers, body, sections, links, related
5. For skill/sub: add real scripts, schema, and depends
6. Validate with [[dojo/validate]]

sections.title: Ecosystem template
sections.body: Ecosystem nodes are top-level namespaces with `parent: null`. Include `more` links to official docs and repo. The body should explain the platform scope, list child standards and skills, describe the API surface, and link to auth/rate-limits context nodes. Ecosystem templates include `more`, `frontmatter`, and `repository` fields.

sections.title: Skill template
sections.body: Skill nodes are executable and must include `scripts[]` with real code, `schema` with input/output contracts, and `depends[]` for cross-node dependencies. The template stubs these with TODO placeholders. Also include `sub[]` if the skill has specialized variants. The SKILL.md file should be created alongside the node.json for portable bundling.

sections.title: Context template
sections.body: Context nodes are knowledge-only — no scripts, no schema, no depends. Include `content_type` (reference, guide, explainer, comparison, changelog, faq, glossary) to categorize the content. The `frontmatter` should include `estimated_reading_time` and `audience`. Context nodes must link to at least one actionable skill node to avoid dead-end knowledge.

sections.title: Sub template
sections.body: Sub nodes are specialized variants of a skill. They must have a skill parent. The template is similar to the skill template but with a more focused scope. Sub nodes inherit the parent skill's tags and should add variant-specific tags. Scripts should handle the specific variant (e.g., deploy-preview vs deploy-production).

sections.title: Template usage guide
sections.body: Copy the template, replace placeholders, derive parent from URI, fill knowledge fields, add scripts for skill/sub, validate. The scaffold command ([[dojo/authoring/scaffold]]) automates this: `dojo scaffold skill github/actions/workflows` creates the directory, writes the skeleton, and creates the SKILL.md. Manual usage is for when you want to customize before scaffolding.

links.context: Scaffold command that uses these templates

related.note: Templates implement the spec's field requirements
Contributing guide references templates for new authors

author: dojo-community
